commit_message: good progress on front end, organized the js files a bit, password and memory reset implemented

user_prompt: to `html\timezone.js` add a IANA timezone string to POSIX TZ string converter lookup table, and a function that gets the current browser time in POSIX TZ string, like the right column of the `timezone_option_groups->zones` table
it has to handle daylight savings time correctly
agent_reply: For DST zones, the lookup returns the table-style POSIX rule strings, for example `America/Los_Angeles -> PST8PDT,M3.2.0,M11.1.0`. If the browser timezone is unmapped and appears to observe seasonal offset changes, the helper returns `""` instead of pretending the current offset is safe forever.
I also regenerated [inc/web_assets.h](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/inc/web_assets.h) via the build.
There are unrelated sprite asset changes already in the working tree; I left those alone.

For DST zones, the lookup returns the table-style POSIX rule strings, for example `America/Los_Angeles -> PST8PDT,M3.2.0,M11.1.0`. If the browser timezone is unmapped and appears to observe seasonal offset changes, the helper returns `""` instead of pretending the current offset is safe forever.
I also regenerated [inc/web_assets.h](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/inc/web_assets.h) via the build.
There are unrelated sprite asset changes already in the working tree; I left those alone.

user_prompt: have the function `function time_sync_onsubmit()` include the result from `get_current_browser_posix_tz_string()`, extend `void timeSync(AsyncWebServerRequest* request)` to accept the `POSIX TZ` string, and set it in `network_cfg_t->timezone` and then save it
agent_reply: Done. `time_sync_onsubmit()` now sends the browser POSIX TZ string as `timezone=...` when `get_current_browser_posix_tz_string()` can provide one, and updates the timezone select from the server response.

On the firmware side, [WebCfgHandlers.cpp](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/WebCfgHandlers.cpp:419) now accepts that POSIX TZ parameter, copies it into `network_cfg_t.timezone`, saves via `WifiManager::replaceConfig()`, and returns the effective timezone in the JSON response.
Both PlatformIO builds passed. `src/version.c` was touched by the builds as usual.

Done. `time_sync_onsubmit()` now sends the browser POSIX TZ string as `timezone=...` when `get_current_browser_posix_tz_string()` can provide one, and updates the timezone select from the server response.

On the firmware side, [WebCfgHandlers.cpp](c:/GitHubRepos/The-Fly/lib/WifiManager/WebCfgHandlers.cpp:419) now accepts that POSIX TZ parameter, copies it into `network_cfg_t.timezone`, saves via `WifiManager::replaceConfig()`, and returns the effective timezone in the JSON response.
Both PlatformIO builds passed. `src/version.c` was touched by the builds as usual.

commit_message: way better time sync from browser, with timezones
